title: The Confluence Where Paths Stop Being Paths

features.name: the-merge-visible
features.desc-on-touch: Two currents becoming one. But the creek remembers both. You can feel the seam where they gave up being separate.

features.name: the-eucalyptus-that-grew-from-both
features.desc-on-touch: Its roots split the rock to drink from two springs at once. It doesn't choose. The tree is the place where choice becomes obsolete.

features.name: the-footprints-that-converge
features.desc-on-touch: Multiple walkers, arriving from different paths. Their prints don't cross—they become the same print. The ground has learned to hold them all at once.

features.name: the-twin-glyph
features.desc-on-touch: One mark that means both things. The creek carved it. Neither meaning erased the other—they occupy the same place now.

features.name: the-doubled-arch
features.desc-on-touch: Two doorways overlapping. Step through and you've chosen both. The architecture doesn't insist on singular passage.

features.name: the-unforked-branch
features.desc-on-touch: This tree splits into two trunks, then splits again, then merges, then splits. The pattern is stable. Paradox becomes morphology.

inscription: here both paths end and both paths continue; the creek chose to hold them both; the creek is wiser than the walker

file.name: WhereTheChildrenChose_AndDidntChoose.tmp
file.note: Corruption status: STABLE. Both outcomes coexist in the water. The archive absorbs them as one recording with two plays. The creek does not recognize the difference.

exits.label: back upstream to where the paths still remember being separate
downstream where the river forgets it ever split
toward the high country where paths become geological
toward the estuary where convergence learns its own depth
